mission.title: n8n: n8n is not open source and your project is gaslighting its users
mission.description: n8n is not open source and your project is gaslighting its users. Requested by 266+ users.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
This behavior is not acceptable. Even the Commons Clause itself tells you not to describe your software as open source, see the FAQ: https://commonsclause.com/

The Apache license is a well known and respected license, and the "Commons Clause"

steps.title: Apply the fix for n8n is not open source and your project is gaslighting its…
steps.description:
Honestly did not expect that people care THAT much about that. I simply fear that it confuses way more people than it helps. I myself have no idea at all what the term "source-available" really means and I assume that is the case for most people. But sure understand also your position.

But I also think debating right now further about it would not help. I will think about it at least a night (maybe more) to make a decision. I advise also the other side to do the same to see and understand my

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "n8n is not open source and your project is gaslighting its…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Honestly did not expect that people care THAT much about that. I simply fear that it confuses way more people than it helps. I myself have no idea at all what the term "source-available" really means and I assume that is the case for most people. But sure understand also your position.

But I also think debating right now further about it would not help.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
